description: Tracks all file changes between Cowork sessions and GitHub pushes. Each session entry records what was added, modified, or deleted — and whether it has been pushed to the live repo.

workflow.step_1: Claude makes changes in HUF-repo/ during a Cowork session
workflow.step_2: Changes are logged here with status 'staged'
workflow.step_3: Peter copies changed files to D:\GitHub\HUF and pushes via GitHub Desktop
workflow.step_4: Peter (or Claude) updates status to 'pushed' with commit hash
workflow.note: If a session ends before push, this manifest tells you exactly what's pending

sessions.label: Initial repo publish
sessions.commit: HUF new Core — March 30, 2026 11:49 AM EDT
sessions.notes: 254 files. First public commit. README original version.

sessions.label: README restructure (ChatGPT front-door design)
sessions.commit: README restructure — pushed by Peter same day
sessions.notes: ChatGPT designed three-box opening. Claude drafted full replacement. 27 internal links verified. Broken kill-test link fixed.

files.summary: Restructured: claims/disclaims/test-protocol at top, CLS and provenance below fold, all links verified

sessions.label: v3 Spectrum Analyzer + Triad Architecture
sessions.commit: HUF new Core — included in S000 initial push (files were already in D:\GitHub\HUF before first commit)
sessions.notes: Both files were already in the local repo folder when the initial 254-file commit was made. Verified live on GitHub. No separate commit needed — content matched.

files.summary: DISP-001 phase display: group phase (cumulative TV + K_eff path), carrier wraps, composition ribbon. Real EMBER carrier data for DE/JP/GB/FR/AU, 2000-2025, 9 fuel types.
ARCH-001: GOV (SEES) / CLS (DOES) / GEO (IS) triad. Aitchison simplex, Fisher metric, square root embedding. Research taxonomy: established / frontier / future tiers.

sessions.label: Staging manifest created
sessions.notes: New process file to track all changes between Cowork sessions and GitHub pushes. First file that is genuinely NEW since the initial publish.

files.summary: Session-based change log with staged/pushed status, pending items queue, and file count tracking.

sessions.label: CoDaWork Packet A v3 (Claude rebuild from ChatGPT v2)

sessions.notes: Claude rebuilt ChatGPT's v2 packet with: (1) proper Unicode math rendering for formulas, (2) explicit null-model caveat on p=0.0016, (3) live GitHub repo link on cover and closing, (4) new Appendix A documenting L2->TV metric correction (METRIC-001). New drafts/codawork-2026/ directory created.

files.summary: CoDaWork contact packet: CoDa primer, MC-4 methods challenge, EMBER case note, metric-correction appendix. Proper math notation throughout.
PDF export of the DOCX for direct sharing.

sessions.label: CoDaWork sample datasets + Copilot language audit
sessions.commit: Pushed by Peter March 30 — confirmed via repo file count
sessions.notes: Copilot's 5 critical warnings checked against v3 packet — all clean (no inevitability, no convergence narrative, no overconfident claims). Built CSV+YAML sample data from real EMBER proportions for 3 countries, CoDa-ready format.

files.summary: 77-row CSV: Germany (26yr), Japan (25yr), UK (26yr), 9 fuel types, proportions summing to 1. Real EMBER data.
YAML metadata: carrier definitions, zero handling policy, intended CoDa analyses, provenance.
Quick-start guide with R and Python examples for loading the compositions.

sessions.label: CoDaWork engagement: full conference preparation suite
sessions.suggested_commit_message: Add CoDaWork 2026 conference preparation suite: email draft, prep guide, ternary diagram, CoDa Explorer, abstract, translator, concepts table, field guide, collective survey
sessions.notes: Egozcue (CoDaWork Scientific Committee Chair) responded same day. Accepted contribution (oral or poster TBD). Gave 4 technical comments: avoid field acronyms, use perturbation not subtraction, CoDa not tied to constant sum, Aitchison norm for concentration. Abstract sent 12:00 PM addressing all 4 points. 283 words, plain text, CoDa vocabulary throughout. Full conference preparation suite built: translator, 26-concept table, do/don't field guide, collective celebration survey.

files.summary: Draft email to codawork2026@coda-association.org. Sent 1:19 AM from PeterHiggins@RogueWaveAudio.com with v3 PDF attached.
Study guide: CoDa concepts (Aitchison geometry, log-ratios, perturbation, zeros, closure), key researchers, likely questions, reading list.
Interactive ternary diagram: Fossil/Nuclear/Renewable sub-composition, 3 countries, 2000-2025. Annotated with Fukushima, German nuclear exit, UK coal collapse.
6-panel CoDa visualization: ternary, time prism (Toblerone), CLR transform, perturbation velocity, ILR balances, Aitchison distance matrix. Interactive country/year controls.
CoDaWork abstract (283 words). Plain text, no citations, CoDa vocabulary. Perturbation-based drift detection on EMBER data. Sent to committee March 31.

Full HUF↔CoDa vocabulary translation table with plain-language explanations. What HUF adds (governance, real-time, portability). What CoDa adds (log-ratios, zeros, coherence, nulls). 51/49 collaboration offer.
Living concept table: 26 entries with HUF perspective, CoDa perspective, and bridge. Concepts 1-17 from session discussion, 18-25 from Grok CoDa deep dive, 26 = opening line.

Field guide: DOs (respect geometry, speak from experience, use their vocabulary, admit gaps), DON'Ts (never dismiss CoDa, never argue with founders, never oversell), hidden traps (R package, Dirichlet, change-point, notation), emergency fallback sentence.
Celebratory survey from all 6 collective members + Peter reacting to Egozcue's response. Count now 7 with Egozcue as first external validator.

README updated with v3 tools, CoDaWork section, archive notes, AI count correction.

sessions.label: CoDa entanglement: error analysis, union thesis, instrument description, collective report, naming fixes
sessions.commit: Pushed by Peter April 3, 2026 — confirmed live at drafts/codawork-2026/
sessions.suggested_commit_message: CoDa entanglement session: 16-error calibration study, union thesis, corrected instrument description, naming fixes, collective report CRPT-007
sessions.notes: Session produced the HUF-CoDa entanglement error analysis (16 error sources), CoDa literature cross-reference, union thesis (THE_UNION.md), corrected instrument description (WHAT_HUF_IS.md with phase discriminator/PLL terminology and BTL triple meaning), framework naming fixes (5 files: HERFINDAHL→HIGGINS, Unified→Unity), and collective report CRPT-007. Edward's AI film workstation spec ($50K) produced but NOT part of HUF repo.

files.summary: 16 error sources mapped across HUF-alone, CoDa-alone, HUF-CoDa union. Detection tests and governance actions for each. The calibration study for the combined instrument.
Maps all error sources against published CoDa literature. 8 addressed, 4 gaps (continuous monitoring), 4 new (E-13 through E-16). Specific paper citations throughout.

CoDaWork 2026 thesis statement. What each brings (8 items each direction), 4 union-only capabilities, full architecture diagram, 4-phase deployment path. One sentence: CoDa+HUF = the instrument that watches compositions move and tells you when to care.

Definitive technical description. BTL = Binaural Test Lab (canonical per RWA-001, Bell Telephone Labs lineage), multichannel coherent detector, HUF-GOV = phase discriminator (not PLL), HUF-CLS = PLL, dual metric, target audiences, loudspeaker-to-universal narrative.
Collective report: CoDa entanglement session. Full audit results, conceptual developments, conference readiness assessment, corpus state.
Title fix: HERFINDAHL UNIFORMITY FRAMEWORK → HIGGINS UNITY FRAMEWORK
Title fix: HERFINDAHL UNIFORMITY FRAMEWORK → HIGGINS UNITY FRAMEWORK
Title fix: HERFINDAHL UNIFORMITY FRAMEWORK → HIGGINS UNITY FRAMEWORK
Title fix: HERFINDAHL UNIFORMITY FRAMEWORK → HIGGINS UNITY FRAMEWORK

Battle card gaps table now shows current status vs what's still needed. Prep guide Q&A answers updated. Coimbra Trim List and CONJ-001 winning horse conjecture filed.

files.summary: One-page bring/keep handy/leave behind for Coimbra. Core posture, spoken introduction, success criteria.
Strategic conjecture: CoDa+HUF+Ramsar as winning horse. Signed compositions next. Complex CoDa later. Disciplined speculation, not assured foundation.
16→17 error count. Dual-metric protocol language added. Matches all other documents.

sessions.label: Copilot collective review integration: consistency verified, conference preparation package, audit trail framing
sessions.suggested_commit_message: Copilot review integration: full consistency verified, 6 conference prep files (presentation script, poster layout, demo script, adversarial panel, room control, ecological demo dataset), CRPT-007 updated with copilot_review
sessions.notes: Copilot completed full consistency verification: repo, analyzer, and codawork-2026 drafts all aligned. Identified audit trail of events (5 types) as HUF's core value proposition for CoDa. Produced comprehensive conference preparation package. Confirmed ChatGPT's 4 final adjustments. Validated white flag posture.

files.summary: 14-slide presentation script with full spoken text (10-12 min). Slide 2 added: The Lineage (BTL physics origin, isotropic ground state, paired-measurement doctrine). Includes white flag opening and 2-minute elevator pitch.
A0 poster layout with ASCII specification. 8 sections: motivation, what HUF is, 17 errors, zero tension, dual metric, analyzer, union thesis, deployment path.
Live demo script (4-5 min) with audience interaction questions. 9 demo steps + 5 audience interactions.
DA-01 through DA-08 devil's-advocate Q&A. Mock adversarial panel rehearsal script. Q&A defense sheet (short form).
Room-control strategy for mathematicians (invariants), engineers (failure modes), skeptics (humility). Universal tactics. Three hardest scenarios.
Engineered demo dataset: 5 carriers (Wetland A-F), 9 years, triggers all 5 event types (drift, disagreement, shock, zero, dimensionality change).

Origin story: from BTL loudspeaker physics to CoDa. Isotropic ground state, constrained energy budget on the simplex, paired-measurement doctrine, audit trail. 'This instrument was not designed for CoDa. It was designed for physics. And the physics turned out to be compositional.'
Added copilot_review section (gold/rough diamonds/tailings). Conference readiness updated with 6 new files. Corpus state updated to projected 291 files. One-sentence summary updated.
S011 entry added. JSON structure fixed (malformed bracket/comma pairs from S009/S010 edits).
